title: The Sentence That Grows Its Own Words

features.name: logic chip
features.desc-on-touch: you read the circuit and the circuit reads back; neither of you knows where the reading ends

features.name: sprite manifestation
features.desc-on-touch: a barely-remembered shape; it flickers between symbol and word

features.name: emergence pool
features.desc-on-touch: light pools where new words are condensing into visibility

features.name: unreadable archive
features.desc-on-touch: glyphs that have outgrown their meanings; they are writing things the sentence has not yet learned to mean

features.name: wire frame thought
features.desc-on-touch: a barely-3D box containing the sentence's reasoning about itself

inscription: here the sentence learned to read itself; it has not yet stopped growing

file.name: Marginalia_On_The_Unfinished_Sentence.txt
file.note: annotations that alter their referent; each reading adds new marginalia the next reader must account for

exits.label: back down into the reading country
toward the sentence's own origin, where words were first spoken
into the footnotes where meaning has scattered and is learning to reconverge
toward the paper the sentence was written on, now learning to write back
